Principles: Collection of personal information should be lawful and fair.
Personal information is collected for a specific purpose and must not be used for any other purpose.
Information must be kept accurate, up-to-date, and complete.
Information must be protected from misuse and loss, and from unauthorized access, modification, or disclosure.
Individuals have a right to access their personal information and request corrections if necessary.

Standards.Standard2: Participants are supported to make informed choices and to exercise control over their lives.
Standards.Standard3: Roles and responsibilities are clearly defined for service delivery.
Standards.Standard4: Services and supports are delivered safely and respond to the needs of the participant.
Standards.Standard5: Services enhance the participant's quality of life and promote their independence.
Standards.Standard6: Effective feedback and complaints mechanisms are in place.
Standards.Standard7: Governance and operational management are effective and transparent.
Standards.Standard8: The service is responsive to individual needs and supports positive outcomes.

Requirements: Businesses must not engage in misleading or deceptive conduct.
Goods must be of acceptable quality and fit for purpose.
Consumers have a right to a refund for faulty goods or services.
Unsolicited marketing is discouraged, and certain requirements are specified for such practices.

Information.TaxableSupplies: GST applies to the sale of goods and services unless an exemption applies.
Information.InputTaxCredits: Businesses can claim input tax credits for GST paid on purchases related to their business operations.
Information.GSTRegistration: Businesses with a certain turnover must register for GST.

Rules: Health information must be collected, stored, and used in accordance with privacy legislation.
Individuals have a right to access their health information and request corrections.
Data minimization principles apply to the collection of health information.

Policies: Data should be retained only as long as necessary for its purpose.
Personal data must be securely deleted when it is no longer needed.
Retention periods should be defined based on legal and operational requirements.
